skills.name: Replace Nav Icon with Brand Logo
skills.description: Replace generic Lucide icon placeholders in sticky nav with the actual brand logo image
skills.problem_solved: Generic icon placeholder used in sticky nav instead of actual brand logo
input_required: nav HTML with data-lucide or icon placeholder
output_produced: All nav instances use real logo img tag
Consistent branding across all page views
steps: Step 1: Search ALL instances of data-lucide icons in nav elements (not just the first)

Step 2: Replace each
with
Step 3: Check every view/section that has its own nav or header
Step 4: Verify with grep for any remaining data-lucide in nav context
gotchas: Sites with multiple views/modals often have a SECOND nav header (e.g. legal modal, inner app view) — always grep for ALL instances
Check for lucide icon in mobile hamburger menu too
skills.reuse_instructions: Grep the entire HTML for data-lucide or icon placeholders in nav/header context. Replace every match with an img tag pointing to the brand logo asset. Verify no instances remain.

skills.name: PWA Manifest Full Optimization
skills.description: Create a fully cross-platform site.webmanifest that passes all PWA install checks on every device
skills.problem_solved: Basic manifest breaks PWA install on custom domains; missing cross-platform fields
output_produced: Installable on iOS, Android, Desktop, ChromeOS
steps: Step 1: Set start_url, id, scope all to '/' for custom domain (NOT /repo-name/ path)
Step 2: Add display_override: ['window-controls-overlay','standalone','minimal-ui','browser']
Step 3: Add prefer_related_applications: false to force install prompt
Step 4: Split any 'any maskable' purpose into TWO entries: one purpose:any, one purpose:maskable
Step 5: Add categories, orientation, screenshots: []
gotchas: GitHub Pages repos use /repo-name/ as start_url — custom domains always use /
Never combine 'any maskable' — they must be separate entries for Android adaptive icons
screenshots array can be empty but must be present for full compliance
skills.reuse_instructions: Copy the manifest template. Replace name, short_name, colors, and icon paths. Set start_url/id/scope to '/' for custom domains. Split icon purposes. Validate with Chrome DevTools > Application > Manifest.

skills.name: Cross-Platform PWA Head Meta Tags
skills.description: Add all required Apple and Android meta tags in HTML head for proper standalone app experience when installed
skills.problem_solved: Missing Apple/Android meta tags prevent proper standalone app experience when installed
output_produced: Proper app name under icon on all platforms
steps: Step 1: Add mobile-web-app-capable + apple-mobile-web-app-capable = yes
Step 2: Add apple-mobile-web-app-status-bar-style = black-translucent (shows content under status bar)
Step 3: Add apple-mobile-web-app-title and application-name with short app name
Step 4: Add apple-touch-icon links for 192 and 512 (keep existing 180x180 separate)
skills.code_pattern: <meta name="apple-mobile-web-app-capable" content="yes"><meta name="apple-mobile-web-app-status-bar-style" content="black-translucent"><meta name="apple-mobile-web-app-title" content="App Name">
gotchas: Do not duplicate 180x180 apple-touch-icon if already present
black-translucent makes content bleed under status bar — ensure top padding on body
skills.reuse_instructions: Add all 7 meta tags to the HTML <head>. Replace app name and icon paths. Test by installing the PWA on iOS Safari and verifying the status bar renders correctly.

skills.name: Stripe Price Map Mode Refactor
skills.description: Refactor flat Stripe price map into structured objects with explicit payment/subscription mode per product
skills.problem_solved: Hardcoded price ID comparison for subscription detection; flat string map is fragile
input_required: Knowledge of which products are subscription vs one-time
output_produced: Explicit mode per product, no string comparisons
steps: Step 1: Convert each map value from 'price_X' to { price: 'price_X', mode: 'payment'|'subscription' }
Step 2: Update redirectToStripe to destructure: var entry = map[id]; var priceId = entry.price; var mode = entry.mode;
Step 3: Verify all downstream code that checks map[id] still works (objects are truthy — safe)
Step 4: Update checkout call: mode: mode instead of ternary
skills.code_pattern: var stripePriceMap = { 'prod_X': { price: 'price_Y', mode: 'payment' } }; var entry = stripePriceMap[productId]; stripe.redirectToCheckout({ mode: entry.mode })
gotchas: Check ALL places that use stripePriceMap — form submit guards, analytics, etc.
Object values are truthy so if (map[id]) checks still work
Subscription mode requires price to be set as recurring in Stripe dashboard
skills.reuse_instructions: Find the existing price map. Convert each string value to { price, mode } object. Update all references to destructure entry.price and entry.mode. Test both payment and subscription checkout flows.

skills.name: Stripe Checkout Error Toast + Recovery Pattern
skills.description: Replace browser alert() on Stripe errors with branded toast notifications and automatic button recovery
skills.problem_solved: browser alert() on payment errors breaks UX; no recovery path for network failures
output_produced: Branded error toast matching site design
steps: Step 1: Create showStripeErrorToast(message) that injects a fixed-position toast matching site colors
Step 2: Create restoreStripeBtn() that re-enables button and restores original text+icon
Step 3: Set button to disabled + spinner text BEFORE calling stripe.redirectToCheckout()
Step 4: In .then(): if result.error → restoreStripeBtn() + showStripeErrorToast()
Step 5: Add .catch() → restoreStripeBtn() + showStripeErrorToast('Unable to connect...')
Step 6: Fix successUrl/cancelUrl to use origin + '/' not origin + pathname (trailing slash bug)
skills.code_pattern: stripe.redirectToCheckout({...}).then(r => { if(r.error){ restore(); toast(r.error.message); }}).catch(e => { restore(); toast('Connection error'); });
gotchas: origin + pathname can produce double slashes or missing slashes — always use origin + '/'
restoreStripeBtn must handle case where button doesn't exist (direct API calls)
Toast needs a unique ID to prevent stacking on rapid retry
skills.reuse_instructions: Add showStripeErrorToast() and restoreStripeBtn() helper functions. Wrap the redirectToCheckout call with .then()/.catch() using both helpers. Style the toast to match the site's existing design system.

skills.name: Cal.com Multi-Recipient Notification Routing
skills.description: Configure Cal.com to notify multiple recipients on bookings, no-shows, and reminders with priority ordering
skills.problem_solved: Cal.com only notifies one email by default; critical leads miss meeting notifications
steps: Step 1: In cal.com: Settings → Teams → Invite primary contact as Admin
Step 2: Event Types → [Event] → Hosts → add primary email first
Step 3: Workflows → New → Trigger: Booking Created → Email action per recipient
Step 4: Add No-Show workflow → Trigger: No Show → Email all stakeholders
Step 5: Add 24h reminder → Trigger: Before Event (24h) → Email guest + team
Step 6: Add webhook endpoint comment in codebase for relay to n8n/custom system
skills.code_pattern: // Cal.com webhook: POST https://your-n8n/webhook/cal → route to luckieg, admin, hero in order
gotchas: cal.com free plan limits Workflows — check plan limits before designing flow
Team host priority only available on Teams plan
Always test with a real booking after setup to verify email order
skills.reuse_instructions: Log into Cal.com dashboard. Follow the steps to add team members, configure host priority, create booking/no-show/reminder workflows, and optionally add a webhook for external relay.

skills.name: Agent System Skills JSON Scaffold
skills.description: Create a structured agent skills registry with triggers, actions, product definitions, and integration mappings
skills.problem_solved: No structured definition of what the company agent can do; workflows can't be assembled without a skill registry
input_required: List of business functions to automate
output_produced: Integration block mapping tools to purposes
steps: Step 1: List all business operations as skills: onboarding, billing, compliance, CRM, content, bookkeeping, overhead
Step 2: For each: define triggers[] (what fires it) and actions[] (what it does)
Step 3: Add product skills for each branded deliverable (Analytics, Blueprints, Checklists)
Step 4: Add integrations{} block mapping each tool to its purpose and env var
Step 5: Create one n8n workflow JSON per core skill as execution template
Step 6: Write README documenting the full system architecture
gotchas: Skills must have concrete triggers and actions — avoid vague descriptions
n8n workflow stubs need real node types (not placeholder names)
Integrations block should reference env vars not hardcoded credentials
skills.reuse_instructions: List all business operations. For each, create a skill object with id, name, description, triggers, and actions. Add product entries for deliverables. Map integrations with env var references. Generate n8n workflow stubs and a README.
